title: Improve LoginPage styling and implement OTP backend functionality

requirements.summary: Refine LoginPage UI styling to improve visual hierarchy, spacing, and responsiveness while maintaining white + blue color scheme
acceptanceCriteria: LoginPage maintains white + blue color scheme
Form elements have consistent spacing and alignment
Buttons use consistent styling with hover states
Layout is responsive and works on mobile devices
Typography hierarchy is clear and readable

file_operations.description: Refine the LoginPage component styling to improve visual hierarchy, spacing, and button consistency. Update the container structure to use consistent padding (e.g., p-8), improve form field spacing with gap utilities, ensure buttons have consistent sizing and hover states using the existing blue color scheme from Tailwind config, add responsive breakpoints for mobile layout, and improve typography hierarchy with appropriate text sizing and weight. Keep the existing three-step flow (mobile entry, OTP verification, signup) intact.

requirements.summary: Update useOTPAuth hook to integrate with backend sendOTP and verifyOTP methods
acceptanceCriteria: useOTPAuth calls backend sendOTP method when sending OTP
useOTPAuth calls backend verifyOTP method during verification
Authentication state is updated based on backend verification response
Error handling for failed OTP operations is implemented
User session is established after successful OTP verification

file_operations.description: Replace the mock localStorage OTP logic with actual backend integration. Import useActor hook to access the backend actor. Update sendOTP function to call actor.sendOTP(mobileNumber) instead of generating and storing OTP locally. Update verifyOTP function to call actor.verifyOTP(mobileNumber, code) and handle the AuthResult response. Set authentication state based on the backend verification result (authenticated flag and userId). Add proper error handling for failed backend calls and return error messages to the UI. Remove the mock OTP generation and localStorage storage logic since the backend now handles OTP management.

Add useMutation hooks for sendOTP and verifyOTP operations. Create useSendOTP mutation that calls actor.sendOTP(mobileNumber) and handles loading/error states. Create useVerifyOTP mutation that calls actor.verifyOTP(mobileNumber, code), returns the AuthResult, and invalidates relevant query cache on success. These mutations will be consumed by the useOTPAuth hook and LoginPage component to provide loading states and error handling.

requirements.summary: Update LoginPage to display loading states during OTP operations and show feedback messages
acceptanceCriteria: Send OTP button shows loading state while request is processing
Verify button shows loading state during OTP verification
Form inputs are disabled during active requests
Error messages are displayed for failed operations
Success feedback is shown before navigation to dashboard

file_operations.description: Update LoginPage to consume the sendOTP and verifyOTP mutations from useQueries. Add loading state handling by checking mutation.isPending for both send and verify operations. Update the Send OTP button to show loading text and disable when isPending is true. Update the Verify button to show loading text and disable during verification. Disable mobile number input during OTP send operation and OTP code input during verification. Display error messages from mutation.error when operations fail. Show a success message briefly after successful OTP verification before the user is redirected to dashboard (the redirect logic is handled by App.tsx protected routes). Ensure form submission handlers call the mutation functions instead of the old hook methods.
